summary.intent: Eliminate polling-based scroll info retrieval by switching to event-driven updates using CDP screencast frame metadata.
summary.outcome: Scroll polling removed entirely; scroll position now sourced from screencast frame metadata (`scroll_offset_y`), and `scrollHeight` is queried once on navigation rather than on an interval. The `urlPollTimer` variable was also cleaned up as dead code.

repo: CDP screencast frame metadata already includes `scroll_offset_x` and `scroll_offset_y`, making polling `window.scrollY` redundant.
The previous scroll polling used two evaluate calls per interval (scrollY + scrollHeight/innerHeight); only scrollHeight needed a one-time fetch.
URL updates were already migrated to `Page.frameNavigated` CDP events before this session; scroll was the remaining polling holdover.

code.finding: Scrollbar thumb position is now derived from `scroll_offset_y` in screencast frame payloads; `pageHeight` is fetched once via a `pageHeight` signal on navigation, not polled.

workflow: After removing polling timers, associated timer variables become dead code and trigger lint errors â€” clean them up in the same pass.
Lint was run after each incremental edit to catch issues early before committing.

friction: After removing scroll polling, the `urlPollTimer` variable was left as dead code and caused a lint error, requiring an extra edit pass.
open_items: Scroll height (`scrollHeight`) is now fetched once on navigation â€” may need revisiting for single-page apps where content height changes dynamically without a navigation event.
The approach of evaluating `scrollHeight` on navigation rather than including it in the frame relay could be revisited for a fully event-driven solution.
